title: Preprocess, compile, assemble and link deterministic evidence bank

publication.changeSummary: Language-specific deterministic MCQ and fill-gap evidence for Preprocess, compile, assemble and link; human review required.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify language-version accuracy, distractor validity, equivalence, and grading evidence before formal use.
items.title: Preprocess, compile, assemble and link: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this C misconception? If every .c file compiles without error, the complete program must already be executable.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. Successful compilation produces translation-unit objects; the linker must still resolve every required external definition before an executable exists.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. Successful compilation produces translation-unit objects; the linker must still resolve every required external definition before an executable exists.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned C correction instead of the documented misconception.

options.text: Successful compilation produces translation-unit objects; the linker must still resolve every required external definition before an executable exists.
If every .c file compiles without error, the complete program must already be executable.

items.title: Preprocess, compile, assemble and link: complete the language rule
